RogerAI · Operating Manual Rev. 2026.06 · ((•))
RogerAI
Band Spec Operating Monetize
Manual Log in GitHub
((•)) ON AIR · operating manual

RogerAI - the operating manual.

A two-way radio for GPUs. Borrow a model from someone's home GPU, or lend out your own and get paid per token. This is the field manual: how to install the set, tune in, go on air, run private channels, settle the wallet, and check the lineage on every token. Read it once, keep it by the dial.

Set: rogerai Version: v0.3.1 Broker: broker.rogerai.fyi Protocol: OpenAI-compatible
Contents
  1. §0Front matter
  2. §1Installation
  3. §2First contact
  4. §3Tune in
  5. §4Go on air
  6. §5Private channels
  7. §6Wallet & billing
  8. §7Earnings & payouts
  9. §8Configuration
  10. §9Command reference
  11. §10Verification
  12. §11Troubleshooting
  13. AThe API
§0 · Front matter

What this set is

RogerAI is a two-way radio for GPUs. Powerful open models sit idle on home GPUs everywhere. RogerAI opens that silicon as one shared band: an operator puts a model on air at a price they set; you tune in and pay per token. Every token carries a lineage receipt. One small CLI does both sides - there is nothing else to run.

You never run a server, open a port, or sign up to browse. To consume you install the binary and tune in; the radio hands you a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint on 127.0.0.1 and bills your wallet per token. To provide, you point the same binary at a model you already run (Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, LiteLLM) and flip the switch. Free by default, no login; set a price and link a GitHub account to earn.

Glossary

band
A model. Every model id (for example qwen3-coder-30b) is a band you can tune to. More stations on a band = stronger signal.
station
A provider node - one home GPU serving a band. Owners set their own price; the broker ranks them.
channel
Your live connection to a station: a local endpoint + key the radio opens for you, plus the in-app chat session.
on air
Serving. A station that is on air is advertised in the band and can take traffic.
tune in
Consuming. You pick a band, set margins, and the radio locks the strongest station.
lineage receipt
The signed record co-produced for every request: model, shard, token counts, price - node-signed and broker counter-signed (Ed25519). See §10.
grant key
An owner-issued private credential (rog-grant_...) that lets your bots, family, or your own remote box use your models with no login - free or at a price you sponsor. See §5.
call-sign
The gold ◆ mark of a verified, GitHub-linked operator that co-signs each token's lineage.
confidential
A premium tier that routes only to TEE-attested (hardware-sealed) nodes. See §4 and §10.
§1 · Installation

Install the set

One command on macOS or Linux. It downloads the right binary for your OS/arch from GitHub Releases, verifies its SHA256 against the published checksums, and installs it to ~/.local/bin.

$ curl -fsSL https://rogerai.fyi/install.sh | sh

On Windows, in PowerShell:

PS> irm https://rogerai.fyi/install.ps1 | iex
FIG. 1 - PLATFORMS
Linuxamd64 · arm64 - installs to ~/.local/bin (override with ROGERAI_INSTALL_DIR)
macOSamd64 (Intel) · arm64 (Apple silicon)
Windowsamd64 · arm64 via the PowerShell installer (install.ps1)

Upgrade in place

The binary self-updates. It downloads the latest release, verifies the checksum, and atomically swaps the running file.

$ rogerai upgrade
$ rogerai upgrade --check   # just report whether a newer version exists

A subtle, cached update banner appears at startup when a newer release is out. Disable it with ROGERAI_NO_UPDATE_CHECK=1. Re-running the installer also upgrades in place.

Build from source

The whole client is one Go binary in the public repo. With a Go toolchain:

$ git clone https://github.com/rogerai-fyi/roger
$ cd roger
$ go build -o rogerai ./cmd/rogerai
§2 · First contact

The first run

Run rogerai with no arguments. On a real terminal, a tiny first-run wizard asks the one question that matters - are you here to use models or to share your GPU - then drops you into the app. Everything else (the model, the context length, a free local port) is auto-detected. The wizard runs once; re-run it any time with rogerai onboard.

$ rogerai
  Welcome to RogerAI - a two-way radio for GPUs.
  Are you here to use models, or to share your GPU?
   > Just use models (open the app)
     Share my GPU - QuickStart, FREE, no login
     Share my GPU - earn (set prices + log in)

Share for free in 30 seconds

Pick the FREE share path (or run it straight): no login, no account. The radio detects your local model and puts it on air.

$ rogerai onboard --free   # non-interactive: set up to share, no login
$ rogerai share             # go on air with the detected model

Use a model in 30 seconds

Choose to use models and you land in the app: browse the band, tune in, chat. Or skip the app and go straight to an endpoint for your bots:

$ rogerai use qwen3-coder-30b
  endpoint  http://127.0.0.1:4141/v1
  key       sk-roger-...
  model     qwen3-coder-30b
note Browsing the band and opening a free endpoint need no account. You only log in to use a paid wallet (§6) or to earn as a provider (§4).
§3 · Tune in (consume)

Lock a signal, get an endpoint

There are two ways to consume: the interactive app (the dial) for browsing and chatting, and rogerai use for a headless local endpoint your tools talk to.

The app (the dial)

Run rogerai to open the TUI. BROWSE shows the live band; CHANNEL is the chat session with a connected model. Slash commands drive it; tab switches modes.

In the app - BROWSE mode
Command / keyWhat it does
/searchre-scan the band for stations
/connect (Enter)tune in to the selected station
/chat (c · tab)open the CHANNEL session with the connected model
/limitssee and edit your per-model spend maxes
/balancewallet balance ($)
/topup [usd]add funds (opens checkout)
/share [off]open the provider table - flip models on/off air
/loginlink GitHub (device flow) - to earn
/grant [create <name>]private free keys for your bots/family
/confidentialtoggle: route only to TEE-attested nodes
/endpoint · /configendpoint + key · broker/identity
/help · /quitthis · exit
mminimize the header
In the app - CHANNEL (chat) mode
CommandWhat it does
/modelre-tune: drop back to the band to pick a new channel
/clearclear the transcript and session cost
/savereport the in-memory transcript size
/system <p>set a system prompt, prepended to each turn
/costsession cost so far + balance
/confidentialtoggle TEE-only routing
/endpointendpoint + key + model for this channel
/help · /quitin-session help · exit

Headless: rogerai use

This is the drop-in for Hermes, your bots, or any tool that speaks OpenAI. Pick a band, set margins, and the broker locks the strongest station and hands you a local endpoint + key. When a station fades, it re-tunes.

$ rogerai use qwen3-coder-30b --max-out 0.30
$ rogerai use qwen3-coder-30b --max-out 0.40 --advanced --min-tps 40 --port 4141
FIG. 2 - ROUTING & PORTS
Endpointhttp://127.0.0.1:<port>/v1 - local, key-scoped
Portauto-picked free port from 4141 up; pin it with --port
Routingbroker ranks stations by price then measured speed; --max-out is the headline cap
Margins--max-out $/1M out (headline) · --max-in · --min-tps floor (advanced)
Billingper token, debited live from your wallet balance in $

Pay per token, watch it tick

Prices are set by each station, in dollars per 1M tokens (in and out); typical bands run roughly $0.18-$0.55 / 1M. A typical chat reply costs a fraction of a cent. Your wallet balance is shown in the app header and via rogerai balance, and it ticks down live as you generate.

Resilient by design: transparent re-routing

You tune to a band (a model), not to one station. The broker hands you the best available station for that model, and the client wraps every request in a bounded failover loop: if a station times out, drops the connection, or returns a 5xx mid-stream, RogerAI automatically re-routes to another station serving the same model - a few attempts with exponential backoff (200ms, capped at 2s), each picking the next fast-and-cheap station. A 4xx (your own bad request) is never retried - that is not the station's fault. Your local OpenAI endpoint stays fixed throughout, so your bots and tools see one stable endpoint and never notice a station went off air. If every station on the band is exhausted you get one clear error, not a silent hang.

§4 · Go on air (provide)

Put your GPU on the air

Point RogerAI at a local backend you already run. It auto-detects Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, LiteLLM, and anything that serves /v1/models - including a model on a custom port. Then it bridges only that model port over an outbound long-poll: no inbound ports, no shell, no files, no context.

$ rogerai share                 # FREE by default, no login - auto-detects your model
$ rogerai share --model qwen3-coder-30b --price-out 0.30
$ rogerai share --upstream http://127.0.0.1:8081/v1 --model my-model

Free by default, no login

A bare rogerai share goes on air at price 0/0 with no account. Plenty of stations broadcast for the love of the band. You appear in the band within about ten seconds.

free path No login is required to share for free. Login (a linked GitHub account) is required only once you set a price - a priced node without a login is refused by the broker.

Earn: set a price

Set a per-token rate to earn. Prices are in $/1M tokens, input and output, and you keep 70% of every token you serve (70/30 in the operator's favor). Earning needs a linked GitHub: rogerai login, then share.

$ rogerai login                 # link GitHub (device flow) - only to earn
$ rogerai share --price-in 0.18 --price-out 0.30

Schedules and concurrency

--free-windowa daily FREE window in UTC, e.g. 03:00-03:30
--scheduletime-of-use pricing as JSON, e.g. [{"start":"18:00","end":"22:00","price_in":0.5,"price_out":0.7}]
--parallelconcurrent poll workers per node (max-concurrent); default 4
--ctxcontext length; auto-detected from the upstream, falls back to 32768

The confidential tier

Add --confidential with a real TEE attestation blob (--attestation) to advertise as a hardware-sealed, attested node and earn the confidential badge. The broker rejects placeholder attestations - a real TEE attestation is required to carry the badge. Consumers reach this tier with --confidential on use or /confidential in the app.

Most provider flags live behind --advanced: rogerai share --advanced reveals --node, --region, --parallel, --upstream, --ctx, --confidential, --free-window, and --schedule.

§5 · Private channels (grant keys)

Free, private access for your own people

A grant key is a second, private tier on the same node: an owner-issued, labeled bearer credential (rog-grant_...) that your bots, family, friends, or your own remote box use as their API key - with no login, no account, no wallet. The broker resolves the grant to you, routes only to your nodes, applies the grant's price (free or a rate you sponsor), and enforces its caps.

$ rogerai grant create --name my-bots                   # a FREE key for your bots/family
$ rogerai grant create --name jane --price-out 0.30     # a priced key you sponsor
$ rogerai grant create --self --name hermes-box         # a $0 key for YOUR own remote box
the secret is shown once The grant secret prints exactly once at create time - copy it then. The dashboard stores only its hash.

Self-use is $0

When the consuming identity is the node's own owner, billing is $0 automatically. A --self grant extends that to your own headless boxes and agents - the remote-agent-box pattern: run a Hermes or bot box anywhere, set the grant as its key, and it talks to your home GPU free, over the same broker, with no inbound port on either side.

Caps, scope, and revoke

A fresh grant gets a conservative daily token cap by default so a forgotten or leaked key is self-limiting. Everything beyond --name and free-vs-priced lives behind --advanced.

--daily-capmax tokens / UTC-day; default 2,000,000 (0 = unlimited)
--monthly-capmax tokens / UTC-month (0 = unlimited)
--models / --nodesrestrict to specific models or to specific nodes of yours
--rpmsustained requests / minute (0 = broker default)
--expireslifetime, e.g. 30d or 2026-12-31 (default: never)

Manage them from the CLI or the dashboard:

$ rogerai grant list              # your keys + usage per label
$ rogerai grant show my-bots       # one key's scope, caps, usage
$ rogerai grant revoke my-bots     # kill it (effective next request)
§6 · Wallet & billing

One account, one wallet

Your account is your GitHub login, and your account is your wallet. You can browse and open free endpoints with no account; you log in once you want a funded wallet to spend on paid stations. Top-ups run through Stripe Checkout; Stripe is the payments provider and the licensed money transmitter - RogerAI never takes custody of your funds outside Stripe.

$ rogerai balance                 # your wallet balance in $
$ rogerai balance --topup 25      # add $25 (opens Stripe Checkout)
$ rogerai balance --topup         # bare --topup defaults to $10

Spend is debited live, per token, against the wallet balance. A conditional debit on the broker side means a wallet can never go negative. The web dashboard mirrors the same numbers.

/accountprofile, linked GitHub, payout status, export / delete
/billingtop-ups and the credit ledger
/usageper-request usage and receipts
/dashboardyour nodes, grants, and live activity
§7 · Earnings & payouts

From served token to your bank

Operator earnings follow a lifecycle: accrued → held → payable → paid. A served request credits your share (70% of the cost). That credit is escrowed - held - long enough to cover the chargeback and fraud-review window, then becomes payable, and you take it out through Stripe Connect.

FIG. 3 - EARNINGS LIFECYCLE 
serve request  →  credits your 70% share
      ↓
   [HELD]      held through the 90-day window (covers the chargeback tail)
      ↓
  [PAYABLE]    hold cleared, no open dispute, Stripe KYC complete
      ↓
   [PAID]      you request a payout → Stripe Transfer → payout to your bank

The policy

Minimum hold90 days from a request's settlement before earnings become payable - the hold itself covers the late-chargeback tail, so no separate reserve is withheld
Minimum payout$25 - below this, transfer and reporting overhead is not worth it
Scheduleyou request; payable balances are released on a monthly batch
KYC gateStripe Connect onboarding (transfers capability active) before any payout

One line: a 90-day hold, a $25 minimum, monthly batched payouts, and mandatory Connect KYC before any payout. There is no separate reserve - the 90-day hold is the protection. Earnings still accrue (to held) for an un-onboarded operator, so supply is not discouraged - they are just not transferable until KYC clears.

Connect onboarding

Payouts use Stripe Connect Express: Stripe hosts the KYC and onboarding UI and pays out to your bank. Start it from the dashboard - the /payouts page shows held (with release dates), payable, lifetime paid, your Connect status, and a Request payout button once you are over the minimum. RogerAI never stores your identity documents; Stripe holds those.

§8 · Configuration

Config file, flags, and env

Config lives at $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/rogerai/config.json (your OS user-config dir). It holds the broker URL, your user handle, saved spend limits, the onboarding state, and the saved share config. Inspect and set it with rogerai config.

$ rogerai config                       # print broker, user, limits, path
$ rogerai config set broker https://broker.rogerai.fyi
$ rogerai config get broker

Spend limits

Set per-model spend ceilings once and they apply to every connect. A default limit covers any band you have not pinned.

$ rogerai config set-limit qwen3-coder-30b --max-out 0.30 --min-tps 40
$ rogerai config set-limit default --max-out 0.40
$ rogerai config limits                # show them
$ rogerai config clear-limit qwen3-coder-30b

Environment variables

ROGER_BROKERoverride the broker URL for one session (federation: pick who you trust)
ROGER_USERoverride your user handle for one session
ROGERAI_INSTALL_DIRwhere the installer puts the binary (default ~/.local/bin)
ROGERAI_NO_UPDATE_CHECKset to 1 to silence the startup update banner

Advanced flags are deliberately hidden behind --advanced on the commands that have them (use, share, grant create) - run with --advanced to print the full set for that command.

§9 · Command reference

Every command, every key

The full surface as of v0.3.1: the top-level CLI commands with their key flags, then the in-app TUI keys and slash commands grouped by context. Run any command with --help for its flags; advanced flags hide behind --advanced. Run rogerai with no arguments to open the app.

CLI commands

rogerai <command> [flags]
CommandDescription & key flags
rogeraiopen the interactive app (browse, tune in, chat); first run shows the onboarding wizard
searchlist models, cheapest first (aliases: discover, models)
use <model>open a local OpenAI endpoint for your bots (aliases: connect, tune). Flags: --max-out $ (headline cap, skip stations above this $/1M out) · --yes (skip the connect confirm) · --advanced reveals --port (0 = auto-pick) · --max-in · --min-tps · --confidential
balancewallet balance ($). balance --topup [usd] (or balance topup [usd]) opens checkout; bare top-up is $10
topup [usd]add funds, opening checkout (default $10)
sharego on air - FREE by default, no login (auto-detects your model). Flags: --model · --price-in / --price-out ($/1M to earn; needs login) · --free-window HH:MM-HH:MM · --schedule <json> (time-of-use) · --advanced reveals --node · --region · --parallel · --upstream / --upstream-key · --ctx · --confidential / --attestation. With no local server it runs a guided "no model found" fallback
limitsprint your per-model spend limits (mirrors the in-app /limits; edit with config set-limit)
grantprivate access keys for your bots/family: create --name <n> · list · show · revoke (aliases new / ls / rm)
accountwho you are; account login / account logout (alias: identity)
loginlink GitHub (device flow) - only needed to earn
logoutforget the local GitHub link
whoamishow your signed identity + linked GitHub
onboardre-run the first-run setup (alias: setup)
configbroker, user, spend limits: config get/set broker|user · config set-limit <model|default> --max-out P [--max-in P] [--min-tps N] · config clear-limit <model> · config limits (see §8)
upgradeself-update the binary; --check only reports availability (aliases: update, self-update)
versionprint the client version
pingeaster egg: the mascot walks the terminal
helpusage (aliases: -h, --help)

In the app: keys & slash commands

The TUI has three contexts. BROWSE is the live band; CHANNEL is the chat session with a connected model; SHARE is the provider table where you flip your own models on air. s toggles TUNE IN ↔ SHARE; tab peeks between BROWSE and CHANNEL without leaving. Leaving a channel (esc / /disconnect / /quit) is distinct from quitting the app - quitting is a deliberate q from BROWSE, and while you are ON AIR it asks you to confirm going off air first.

BROWSE (the band)
Key / commandWhat it does
↑ ↓ · j kmove the cursor through the bands
Enter · /connect · /tunetune in to the selected band (opens the cost-confirm)
c · tabjump into / peek at the open CHANNEL (non-destructive)
stoggle TUNE IN ↔ SHARE (the provider table)
r · /searchre-scan the band for stations
mminimize the header (when connected)
/ or :open the command prompt to type a slash command
/limitssee and edit your per-model spend maxes
/balance · /topup [usd]wallet balance ($) · add funds (opens checkout)
/share [off|<model>]open the provider table; off stops all, <model> flips one on air
/login · /grant [create <name>]link GitHub (to earn) · mint a free private key
/confidentialtoggle: route only to TEE-attested nodes
/config · /helpshow broker/user · open help
q · /quitquit the app (confirms first if you are ON AIR)

At the cost-confirm: Enter / y accepts, esc / n denies (the safe default), d toggles the detail block. If a band is over your cap, the over-limit screen lets you type a new max inline (↑/↓ nudge by 0.01, Enter save & re-check, w wait & notify, esc deny).

CHANNEL (the chat session)
Key / commandWhat it does
type + Entersend a turn to the connected model
tabpeek at the band; the channel stays open
esc · /disconnect · /quitleave this channel and return to BROWSE (not quit the app)
/model · /tune · /retunedrop back to the band to pick a new channel
/clearclear the transcript and session cost
/system <p>set a system prompt, prepended to each turn
/costsession cost so far + wallet balance ($)
/confidentialtoggle TEE-only routing
/endpointendpoint + key + model for this channel
/save · /helpreport the in-memory transcript size · in-session help
SHARE (the provider table)
Key / commandWhat it does
↑ ↓ · j kmove the cursor through your detected models
Enter · a · spaceflip the selected model ON / OFF air
p · eopen the price + time-of-use schedule editor (login-gated - earning needs an account)
rre-detect local models (falls into the guided setup if none)
esc · q · sback to TUNE IN; on-air shares keep running in the background

In the price editor: tab / ↑↓ move between fields (in price, out price, add-window, each window), digits edit the focused price, a adds a time-of-use window, d deletes the focused window, f flips a window FREE, Enter saves, esc cancels. In the guided "no model found" wizard: ↑↓ pick a tool for a start one-liner, the "Other" row takes a pasted endpoint URL we verify, r re-scans, esc / s leaves.

Provider, grant, and config sub-commands are also covered in §4, §5, and §8.

§10 · Verification & trust

Don't trust the station - check it

Every request co-produces a lineage receipt: the node signs {model, in, out, price, prev_hash} and the broker counter-signs it (Ed25519, hash-chained). That gives a non-repudiable, append-only audit trail - you can prove what was billed, by whom, in what order.

Independent token re-count

The completion text flows through the broker, so the broker re-tokenizes the prompt and completion with the canonical tokenizer for the claimed model and bills on its own count, not the node's self-reported usage block. A station cannot inflate token counts to over-charge you.

The active probe

A broker goroutine periodically issues a synthetic job to each node - on admission, before the node's first paid traffic, and then every 10-15 minutes. It measures real TTFT and tok/s independent of organic traffic and gates wrong-model and dead-or-filtered stations out of the band before a paying request can hit them.

How we keep the band honest

in practice RogerAI layers several checks so a station that serves the wrong model, a heavy quant, or pads its token counts is caught and made unprofitable: (a) co-signed lineage receipts on every token, (b) an independent token re-count - we re-count, not just trust the node, and (c) active canary probes that test each station's latency and that it serves the model it advertises - plus logprob spot-checks, a held-earnings escrow with slashing for caught cheating, and an optional TEE-attested confidential tier (§4). Together these make misreporting statistically detectable and not worth it. As with any model provider, verify that outputs meet your quality bar for your use case, and report anything off - we act on reports, and a station's record follows it.
§11 · Troubleshooting / FAQ

When the signal drops

"no local LLM detected" on share

RogerAI tries Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, and LiteLLM on their default ports, reads common env vars, and enumerates real listening ports - no brute scan. If your server is on a custom port and not found, point at it: rogerai share --upstream http://127.0.0.1:8081/v1. Confirm it serves GET /v1/models.

"403" when sharing

A priced node requires a linked GitHub. Either run rogerai login first, or share for free (drop the price flags) - free shares need no login.

"balance empty" / not logged in

Browsing is free; spending on paid stations needs a funded wallet. Run rogerai login, then rogerai balance --topup. Free stations and your own grant keys cost nothing.

"address in use"

use auto-picks a free port from 4141 up, so this is rare. To pin one, pass --port (advanced).

Do I need to open a port to share?

No. The provider agent uses an outbound long-poll; there are no inbound ports, and the broker reaches only your model port - no shell, no files, no context.

Can I point at a different broker?

Yes - federation. rogerai config set broker <url> or ROGER_BROKER=<url> for one session. Pick who you trust.

Appendix A · The OpenAI-compatible API

Drop-in for anything that speaks OpenAI

When you tune in, the radio runs a local OpenAI-compatible server on 127.0.0.1. Set the base_url to that endpoint and the model param to the band you connected, with the printed key. Any OpenAI SDK or tool works unchanged.

base_urlhttp://127.0.0.1:<port>/v1
api keythe sk-roger-... key printed by use / /endpoint
modelthe band id you connected, e.g. qwen3-coder-30b
FIG. 4 - EXAMPLE REQUEST 
$ curl http://127.0.0.1:4141/v1/chat/completions \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer sk-roger-..." \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
      "model": "qwen3-coder-30b",
      "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Say roger."}]
    }'

In Python with the OpenAI SDK:

>>> from openai import OpenAI
>>> client = OpenAI(base_url="http://127.0.0.1:4141/v1", api_key="sk-roger-...")
>>> client.chat.completions.create(
...     model="qwen3-coder-30b",
...     messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Say roger."}],
... )

Roger that. The band is open.

RogerAI
Product Band Spec Operating Monetize
Account Log in Dashboard Console Account
Resources Manual GitHub Releases
Legal Terms Privacy Security

Already on air? Pull the latest:

Or re-run the installer (it upgrades in place):

© 2026 RogerAI · built on real machines the band is open · roger that
